Arguments: <data> - collection or single model
Returns: last id inserted or updated

methods.name: scrapeanything.database.repository.Repository.update
documentation:
Description: updates an entity from its version on the database
Arguments: 
- <entity_from_database> - entity loaded from the database
- <entity> - entity built in memory
Returns: updated entity (holds Id from the database saved entity)

methods.name: scrapeanything.integration.excel.Excel.append_to_excel
documentation:
Append a DataFrame [df] to existing Excel file [filename]
into [sheet_name] Sheet.
If [filename] doesn't exist, then this function will create it.

@param filename: File path or existing ExcelWriter

: (Example: '/path/to/file.xlsx')

@param df: DataFrame to save to workbook
@param sheet_name: Name of sheet which will contain DataFrame.

> (default: 'Sheet1')

@param startrow: upper left cell row to dump data frame.

: Per default (startrow=None) calculate the last row
  in the existing DF and write to the next row...

@param truncate_sheet: truncate (remove and recreate) [sheet_name]

: before writing DataFrame to Excel file

@param to_excel_kwargs: arguments which will be passed to `DataFrame.to_excel()`

: [can be a dictionary]

@return: None

Usage examples:

```pycon
>>> append_df_to_excel('d:/temp/test.xlsx', df)
```

```pycon
>>> append_df_to_excel('d:/temp/test.xlsx', df, header=None, index=False)
```

```pycon
>>> append_df_to_excel('d:/temp/test.xlsx', df, sheet_name='Sheet2',
                    index=False)
```

```pycon
>>> append_df_to_excel('d:/temp/test.xlsx', df, sheet_name='Sheet2',
                    index=False, startrow=25)
```

3. \[MaxU\](<https://stackoverflow.com/users/5741205/maxu?tab=profile>)

methods.name: scrapeanything.utils.utils.Utils.slugify
documentation:
Converts a string into a slug, which is a URL-safe string that is
typically used in URLs.

Args:
    value (str): The string to convert into a slug.

Returns:
    str: The slugified string.
